meta.title: BitPoint vs iCard — Definitive Distinction
meta.source: RECALL Block 353 + WhatsApp confirmation to Henry Leong & Michael

bitpoint.definition: A unit of knowledge made physical, tradeable, collectible. Not cryptocurrency. Not blockchain. A BIT of POINT. Our bitcoin exchange made tangible.
bitpoint.rule: Every observation becomes a card. Every card becomes a BitPoint.
bitpoint.format: Collectible card — numbered, tradeable, part of the 52-card set
bitpoint.purpose: The product. External-facing. Shared with partners, investors, players.
bitpoint.example: BitPoint 01 of 52 — THE BITPOINT (Physical Intellectual Currency card shared with Henry & Michael)

icard.definition: An infographic snapshot card for internal project use. Status reports, review findings, data summaries, return briefs.
icard.format: Dark navy (#0a1628) infographic card with gold (#ffd700) accents
icard.purpose: The process tool. Internal-facing. Project management, decision support, audit trail.
icard.example: iCard #1 — Review Findings (12 university scores snapshot)

key_distinction: BitPoints are the PRODUCT (collectible knowledge cards for the market). iCards are the PROCESS TOOL (internal infographic snapshots for project management). Never interchange them.

tre_correction.correct: TRE = The Reality Engine (Guided Learning Platform)
tre_correction.incorrect: TRE = The Relay Experience (WRONG — was an error in the ALAN & DAVID paper, fixed 2026-03-06)
tre_correction.verification: Zero instances of 'Relay Experience' remain in source files as of 2026-03-06
